methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $39.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.5B - $14.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in AI & Compute

private.name: Skild AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series C), post-money $14B+

private.name: Luma AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series C), post-money $4B

private.name: Runway

private.name: Magic AI
private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.5B+

data.narrative:
Figure AI, a Series C AI & Software company, commands a $39.0B post-money valuation following $1B+ in cumulative funding. The valuation analysis triangulates against a comp universe of 9 private peers and 1 public comparable (Palantir).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression modeling applies a 1.7x multiplier typical of Series C-to-next-stage step-ups, implying $66.3B. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same-stage companies—Skild AI ($14.0B), Luma AI ($4.0B), Runway ($1.5B), and Magic AI ($1.5B)—yields a $2.8B median with a range of $1.5B to $14.0B. The substantial dispersion reflects valuation heterogeneity within early-stage AI, where comparable companies span nearly a 10x range.

Triangulation of both methods produces a range of $2.3B (low) to $76.2B (high), with a median of $34.5B. The current $39.0B valuation sits modestly above the triangulated median, within the upper quartile of the range. Material uncertainty derives from the wide peer variance—stage-comparable multiples do not compress tightly—and the nascent stage of the sector, where revenue scales and unit economics remain unvalidated across the comp set. The gap between peer-median and stage-progression results particularly underscores disagreement on appropriate stage-based advancement versus relative-value positioning.
